James Nevin and Chris Hersom of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiffs.

Joseph Adams of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum representing JT Thorpe & Son, Inc. and Parker Hannifin.
Ian Dillon of Walsworth Franklin representing JM Manufacturing Company, Inc.

Defendant Crane Co. has resolved out of all of the cases. Brayton Group 578 (LC #274139, Teale case - wrongful death) will go to trial first.

At 3:14 p.m., court and counsel confer on the juror questionnaire to be used for hardship as well as a proposed joint statement of the case. There are no motions in limine that need to be heard before voir dire. Juror hardships will be conducted 12/14/11, voir dire on 12/19/11 and 12/20/11, motions in limine will be heard on 1/3/12 and trial will commence on 1/4/12.

The court recess from 3:24 p.m. to 3:37 p.m. in order for counsel to finalize the juror questionnaire.

Court and counsel confer on the questionnaire. Copies of the finalized version must be provided to the court by tomorrow (12/14/11) at 9:00 a.m. in Department 602. Counsel have also finalized a generic statement of the case. Counsel estimate the trial will last six weeks including deliberations. There will be 20 days worth of evidence. All counsel are ordered present for jury hardshipping. Plaintiffs' counsel is allotted one hour for voir dire and defense counsel will confer on possibly conducting a joint voir dire with their time.

Court and counsel confer on deposition designation and counter-designations. The court orders a briefing schedule on it. Counsel in living asbestos cases should also abide by all deadlines set out in today's proceedings.

At 3:57 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

Off Calendar Oct-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth R. Bain, Esq., of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party NACCO Materials Handling Group, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Walter Morris

RULING:
DEFENDANT NACCO MATERIALS HANDLING GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant NACCO Materials Handling Group, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. As to the summary judgment motion, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and the deposition of Mr. Libby create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Hyster brakes) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff has dismissed his false representation cause of action and his claim for punitive damages, and those were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tatiana Oseroff, Esq., of GORDON & REES (619-696-6700), appearing via CourtCall for defendant / moving party Decker Auto Supply, Inc.
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Walter Morris

RULING:
DEFENDANT DECKER AUTO SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Decker Auto Supply, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product sold by defendant or that any such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. Defendant's failure to demonstrate that plaintiff responded to written contention discovery precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
